title: jailrun provider registry data files
description: Validates the providers/*.json data files that hold the substitution lookup tables consumed by probe(S2) and bakery(S4): provider-map.json, pkg-artifacts.json, port-artifacts.json, multi-binary-pkgs.json, coreutils-flavor.json. Each file's shape is one of the $defs below, picked by filename (see .github/scripts/validate_provider_data.py). schema_version is a plain integer today (bump only on a breaking shape change); values stay plain strings/lists for now, but the object wrapper leaves room for richer per-entry metadata (e.g. evidence/verification level) later without a format break.

provider_map.title: provider-map.json
provider_map.description: Linux binary basename -> FreeBSD provider string. Mirrors probe.py's former PROVIDER_MAP.

providers.description: key: basename (lower-case) of the Linux binary. value: pkg:<name> | port:<origin> | build:<recipe-id>

pkg_artifacts.title: pkg-artifacts.json
pkg_artifacts.description: pkg name -> installed artifact path under /usr/local. Mirrors bakery.py's former PKG_ARTIFACTS.

artifacts.description: key: pkg name, e.g. 'cmake'. value: absolute artifact path on freebsd-host.

port_artifacts.title: port-artifacts.json
port_artifacts.description: port origin -> installed artifact path. Mirrors bakery.py's former PORT_ARTIFACTS.

artifacts.description: key: port origin, e.g. 'devel/xtensa-esp-elf'. value: absolute artifact path on freebsd-host.

multi_binary_pkgs.title: multi-binary-pkgs.json
multi_binary_pkgs.description: pkg names that install MANY distinctly-named binaries rather than one matching the package name. Mirrors bakery.py's former MULTI_BINARY_PKGS.

coreutils_flavor.title: coreutils-flavor.json
coreutils_flavor.description: Descriptive metadata about EXISTING provider-map.json entries for coreutils-class binaries (classic Unix text/file utilities where GNU and BSD implementations have historically-known flag/behavior differences, per ROADMAP.md's 0.3 'Substitution policy per entry'). key: a binary basename that MUST already be a key in provider-map.json's `providers` object. value: which userland flavor provider-map.json's CURRENT mapping for that binary actually resolves to today (gnu = the provider string is a GNU-flavored port/pkg, e.g. pkg:gtar; bsd = base-system or a non-GNU port). This file does NOT add or change any binary->provider mapping — it only labels mappings that already exist. See docs/GNU-VS-BSD-POLICY.md.

flavors.description: key: basename (lower-case) of the Linux binary, already present as a key in provider-map.json's providers map. value: gnu | bsd.
